label: Live Socket
description: Turn this program into a live WebSocket endpoint. An outside caller opens a WebSocket at the project's gateway URL and holds a full two-way conversation: nodes downstream send and receive messages via the caller handle. Fires a fresh execution per connection.

outputs.name: started
outputs.description: Fires true once when a socket opens, to kick downstream work. The caller itself is reached via the caller handle, not this port.

fields.label: Path
fields.description: Route suffix under the gateway. Leave empty for the project's root live URL. Must not start with '/'.

fields.label: Require API key
fields.description: When on, the caller must present a minted API key to open the connection. The plaintext is shown once on the node inspector.

fields.label: May outlive the caller
fields.description: Off (default): the run is tied to the caller. If a node hits a long durable wait it holds the worker briefly then the run is killed; a caller disconnect cancels the run. On: the run may suspend and resume later without the caller (it becomes a background job), and a disconnect does not kill it.

fields.label: Hold seconds before giving up on a wait
fields.description: For a caller-tied run: how long a node's wait holds the worker (keeping the connection alive) before giving up. Per-wait overrides win over this. Never unbounded.

then.message: LiveSocket '{id}' path starts with '/'. The gateway prefixes the route automatically; drop the leading slash.
